summary: Decomp/matching PR focused on `src/melee/vi/vi0501.c` and `src/sysdolphin/baselib/psdisp.c`. It implemented `un_8031D9F8` in the VI character/demo path, matched `particleSort`, and substantially improved `psDispParticles` by factoring point and point-trail particle rendering helpers plus many GX/display-state details. The decomp.dev bot reported 2 new matches and 4 unmatched-item improvements: `particleSort` reached 100%, `un_8031D9F8` reached 98.83%, and `psDispParticles` improved from 39.03% to 58.06%. `target_funcs.txt` was removed; its contents were unrelated completed mncharsel targets.

key_files.role: Implemented `un_8031D9F8`, added required includes, added static `HSD_GObj* un_804A2E98[4]`, and wired camera, stage, item, player, JObj animation, and audio setup for the VI/demo scene.
Matched `particleSort` and improved `psDispParticles`; introduced inline wrappers/helpers including `getColorMatAmb_inner`, `getColorMatAmb_noinline`, `psDispSubPoint`, and `psDispSubPointTrail`.
Deleted a temporary target list containing completed `src/melee/mn/mncharsel.c` functions.

what_changed: `un_8031D9F8` replaced an unmatched stub marker with a concrete implementation taking `CharacterKind char_kind`, `costume`, `spawn_mode`, and `spawn_count` parameters.
`vi0501.c` now includes dependencies from camera, ftDemo, ground/stage, item, mp collision, player, GObj, and JObj headers to support the implemented demo setup.
`un_8031D9F8` initializes camera/collision/stage/item/player systems, creates the main demo player, then loops over slots 1 through 3 to create Kirby demo entities and stores them in `un_804A2E98`.

The VI implementation uses `GET_JOBJ`, `HSD_JObjReqAnimAll`, `HSD_JObjAnimAll`, and `HSD_JObjGetTranslation2` to sample animation translation at frame 140, scale it by `1.4285715f * un_804D6F80`, and write positions from `grLib_801C9A10() + 1`.
`un_8031D9F8` ends by calling several `lbAudioAx_*` routines, including `lbAudioAx_80026F2C(0x1C)` and `lbAudioAx_8002702C(0xC, 0x80000004000)`.
`particleSort` was simplified from manually unrolled bucket concatenation into two loops over buckets `0..7` and `8..15`, while preserving separate head/tail chaining behavior.
`particleSort` introduced a `new_var` temporary in the assignment `temp_r29 = (new_var = &hsd_804D0908[arg0]);`, likely to influence register/codegen for matching.
`psdisp.c` added small inline wrappers around `getColorMatAmb`, apparently to shape inlining/call behavior in the larger particle display function.

`psdisp.c` added `psDispSubPoint`, which batches compatible point particles into buffers of 16 positions, sets point size with `GXSetPointSize`, chooses vertex descriptors based on `DispTexture`, and emits `GX_POINTS` through `GXWGFifo`.

`psdisp.c` added `psDispSubPointTrail`, which batches compatible trail particles into 16-entry position/color buffers, computes previous positions from velocity or `calcTornadoLastPos`, applies trail alpha, and emits `GX_LINES` through `GXWGFifo`.

`psDispParticles` moved/retained a cached pointer to `HSD_PSDisp_804D0FC0`, updates viewing/projection matrices, and contains revised calculations for trail/DirVec particle angles, perspective cases, billboard axes, and rotation via `PSMTXRotAxisRad`.
A temporary `target_funcs.txt` containing checked-off mncharsel targets was deleted.
smart_moves: Converted long unrolled bucket-linking code in `particleSort` into bounded `for` loops while still matching, making the matched code easier to read and maintain.
Used small inline/noinline-style wrapper functions around `getColorMatAmb` to control compiler behavior without changing the public logic.
Split repeated point and line/trail batching behavior into `psDispSubPoint` and `psDispSubPointTrail`, exposing the 16-particle batching pattern and the exact GX FIFO writes needed for matching.
Preserved low-level GX state details such as cached point/line widths, vertex descriptor selection, `GXBegin` primitive/fmt/count combinations, and explicit `GXWGFifo` field ordering.

For `un_8031D9F8`, matched a broad setup routine by using real subsystem calls and typed enums/objects where available (`CharacterKind`, `HSD_GObj`, `HSD_JObj`, `Gm_PKind_Demo`, `CKIND_KIRBY`) rather than leaving everything as raw integers.
decomp_lessons: For baselib particle display code, matching may require preserving exact GX FIFO write order and primitive counts even when the C looks repetitive.
Batching temporary stack buffers of size 16 can be an important structural clue for particle rendering functions; here both point and point-trail helpers flush when `count == 16`.

When decompiling particle chains, compatibility predicates matter: size, `appsrt`, selected `kind` bits, color counters, texture/display flags, lighting/material/ambient fields, and trail flags all influence when a batch can continue.
Replacing manual unrolled chains with loops can still match if variable lifetimes and tail-pointer assignments are arranged carefully.
Artificial-looking temporaries such as `new_var`, `pad` locals, or tiny inline wrappers may be legitimate matching tactics for register allocation, stack layout, or inlining shape.

Large setup functions in menu/demo/VI code often need many subsystem headers before a typed implementation is possible; include churn can be evidence of moving from placeholder to real implementation.
Animation sampling can be used as data generation: this PR samples Kirby JObj translation at a specific animation frame, scales by a ground/stage factor, and stores stage positions.
naming_conventions: Still-unknown symbols retain decomp address-style names such as `un_8031D9F8`, `un_804A2E98`, `un_804D6F80`, and `HSD_PSDisp_804D790C`.
Existing project subsystem prefix conventions are used: `Camera_`, `Ground_`, `Stage_`, `Item_`, `Player_`, `ftDemo_`, `lbAudioAx_`, `grLib_`, `HSD_JObj`, and `GX`.

Enum/constant names in the diff use established all-caps or subsystem naming such as `PURA`, `CKIND_KIRBY`, `Gm_PKind_Demo`, `DispTexture`, `DispPoint`, `DispLighting`, `Trail`, `Tornado`, `DirVec`, and `PrimEnv`.

Local variable names in difficult matching code remain pragmatic and compiler-shape oriented (`var_r3`, `var_r5`, `temp_r29`, `sp7B4`, `cache`, `new_var`) when stronger semantic names are not yet evident.
The PR did not provide review discussion explaining naming choices; naming observations come from the diff only.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK(4)` plus explicit unused `s32 pad0` through `pad4` locals in `un_8031D9F8`, indicating stack-layout matching pressure.
Introduced `new_var` solely through a chained assignment in `particleSort`, likely to force the compiler to keep a specific value/lifetime.
Added nested inline wrappers around `getColorMatAmb` to influence inlining without semantically changing color calculation.
Replaced manually expanded bucket handling with two fixed loops over bucket ranges `0..8` and `8..16`, preserving the split between two particle lists.
Used exact floating constants visible in the diff, including `0.7f`, `1.4285715f`, `42.5f`, `255.0f`, `6.0f`, and `1.5707964f`.
Maintained direct `GXWGFifo` writes instead of abstracting vertices, which is often necessary for matching GX display code.
Used `__frsqrte` refinement in the later `psDispParticles` diff excerpt for vector length handling, reflecting low-level floating-point matching constraints.
Decomp.dev metrics provide evidence of success: `particleSort` reached 100%, `psDispParticles` improved by 2756 bytes, and `un_8031D9F8` improved by 640 bytes to 98.83%.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported matched code increased to 69.20% (+1048 bytes) and matched data to 41.20% (+16 bytes).
Reported improvements in `psDispParticles`, `un_8031D9F8`, `vi0501` `.sdata2`, and `psdisp` `extabindex`, plus a small `psdisp` `.sdata2` regression.
No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that finish `un_8031D9F8` from 98.83% to 100%.
Look for later PRs that continue `psDispParticles` after this jump from 39.03% to 58.06%.
Search for other `psdisp.c` PRs using inline wrappers or `new_var` temporaries to force matching.
Check whether `un_804D6F84` is later typed as `ViCharaDesc` as hinted by the existing todo.
Search for PRs that rename `un_804A2E98` or other VI demo globals once their semantics are understood.
